exemplar.title: To what extent does listening to music while learning affect working memory in university students?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does listening to music while learning affect working memory in university students?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear and engaging communication of the topic and focus throughout the essay
Well-formulated, focused research question consistently linked back to the analysis
Effective overall structure and signposting that guide the reader through the argument
weaknesses: No dedicated methodology section describing search strategy, inclusion criteria or data-handling procedures
Introduction ends abruptly mid-sentence, disrupting conceptual flow
Limited justification of methodological choices (e.g., lack of repeated-measures design)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student communicates the topic and research question exceptionally well and maintains a coherent focus throughout, the absence of an explicit methodology section and only partial explanation of methodological choices constrain full marks. Overall performance aligns with the upper half of the 5â6 band, meriting 5/6.

strengths: Consistent, accurate use of cognitive-psychology terminology (e.g., phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad)
Excellent integration of primary and secondary sources to substantiate points
Depth of subject knowledge demonstrated in precise definitions and coherent background synthesis
weaknesses: Minor citation inaccuracies and one mis-dating of a source
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an outstanding grasp of subject-specific concepts and applies sources coherently and accurately throughout. Minor stylistic slips do not undermine the overall excellence, justifying full marks in this criterion.

strengths: Good breadth of research consistently tied to the research question, including seminal and recent empirical studies
Clear comparative analysis of conflicting findings (e.g., support vs. critiques of the Mozart Effect)
Reasoned synthesis of methodological strengths and limitations, with ethical and cultural considerations noted
weaknesses: Statistical depth is limitedâfew effect sizes, p-values or variance measures are reported
Some analytic points remain qualitative rather than quantitatively substantiated
Conclusion cautious and lacks a fully decisive, integrative evaluation of all evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves well beyond description into reasoned evaluation and synthesis, placing this work solidly in the 7â9 band. Shortcomings in statistical rigor and a somewhat tentative final conclusion prevent allocation of top-band marks, meriting 7/12.

strengths: Logical overall structure with all major sections present and appropriately ordered
Inclusion of page numbers and word count demonstrates attention to formal requirements
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting, line-breaks in URLs and leftover LaTeX tags disrupt polish
Bold-tag remnants and uneven reference layout indicate sloppy formatting
Lack of a formal methodology heading in the table of contents detracts from navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essayâs structure is sound, pervasive layout and formatting inconsistencies (broken citations, LaTeX artefacts, variable styles) significantly detract from presentation quality. The work sits in the lower half of the 1â4 band, warranting 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical vocabularyâcentral executive, articulatory suppression, ecological validity, visuospatial sketchpadâ is deployed accurately and in context. Terminology supports, rather than obscures, the argument, and concepts from cognitive psychology and neuroscience are integrated coherently. Isolated informal phrases (e.g., âtouching musicâ) are rare, so overall use is accurate and consistent.

The essay includes page numbers, word count and a reference list, but formatting inconsistencies persist: citation styles vary, URLs break across lines, and occasional LaTeX artefacts and bold-tag remnants disrupt polish. These issues indicate that layout considerations were applied inconsistently, fitting the middle band for this strand.

Across sections the student demonstrates excellent grasp of cognitive-psychology constructs (phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, Mozart Effect, neural correlates). Studies are summarised accurately and used to substantiate points rather than decorate prose. Occasional inaccuracies (e.g., âexcruciatingly vitalâ) and mis-dating of Giannouli et al. are minor and do not detract from an otherwise consistently informed application of sources.

The essay cites a broad range of primary and secondary sources and comments briefly on sampling, controls and validity, indicating some informed selection. However, there is still no dedicated methodology section describing the search strategy, inclusion criteria or data-handling procedures, which limits transparency and replicability. Consequently, the work meets the descriptor for a mostly complete but not fully explicit methodology.

The student introduces the phenomenon of music-supported studying in an engaging manner, defines working-memory terminology accurately, and repeatedly returns to the stated aim of evaluating effects on recall. Clear section headings and signposting (âThis topic interests me becauseâ¦â) keep the reader oriented and demonstrate a coherent thread from introduction through conclusion. Minor lapses in expression (e.g., unfinished sentences, occasional digressions) do not substantially hinder comprehension. Overall, topic, purpose and focus are communicated accurately and effectively.

The bibliography spans seminal and recent empirical articles, meta-analyses and theoretical pieces, most of which are clearly pertinent to the research question. Each studyâs relevance is explained, and cultural, ethical and methodological limitations are often noted. Minor citation errors and some reliance on secondary summaries prevent a top-band award but the overall standard aligns with âgoodâ to âexcellentâ research quality and relevance.

The research question â âTo what extent does listening to music while learning affect working memory in university students?â â is explicitly stated on the title page and revisited throughout the essay when interpreting each study. Discussion consistently links empirical findings back to this question, showing a tight conceptual connection. Although the age group restriction (university students) could be foregrounded more consistently, the question remains focused, manageable and central to the argument, fulfilling the highest-band descriptor.

All required structural componentsâtitle page, abstract substitute, table of contents, clearly labelled sections and conclusionâare present and logically ordered. The flow of headings mirrors the argumentative trajectory, aiding navigation. Although the absence of a formal methodology section is a content issue, the overall structure aligns with EE conventions, warranting the upper band for structure appropriateness.

The discussion synthesises positive and negative evidence and recognises limitations (e.g., laboratory settings, age-group bias). Ethical and cultural considerations are acknowledged, and suggestions for future research are offered. However, the final conclusion stays cautious and does not fully integrate all strands into a decisive evaluation, so the argument, while reasoned and partly critical, falls short of the sustained, incisive critique required for the top band.

Analysis moves beyond description to compare findings (e.g., contrasting support for and critiques of the Mozart Effect) and to relate neural evidence to behavioural data. The student identifies moderators such as musical preference and genre, and discusses methodological strengths/weaknesses. Nevertheless, statistical depth is limitedâeffect sizes and p-values are rarely providedâand some claims remain qualitative, placing the work in the 7â9 band rather than the highest.

annotations.comment: The note that the Mozart effect does not extend past 15 minutes is unsupported by detailed statistical thresholds or references to variance explained, undermining the strength of this temporal limitation.
Several result paragraphs display unrendered <math> tags, indicating formatting issues that compromise the presentation of statistical data and must be corrected.
The table of contents omits a dedicated methodology section, reducing transparency and making it harder for readers to locate information on research procedures and selection criteria.
The explanation of the Mozart Effect study design and conditions is clear and technically accurate, illustrating effective use of psychological terminology and empirical description.
Including authoritative sources such as Encyclopedia Britannica and key neuroscience texts on the final page underscores thorough scope and depth of background research.
The analysis of Franklin et al.âs findings omits effect size discussion and detailed sample information, limiting insight into statistical power and the generalizability of the results.

The section on replication by Nantais and Schellenberg lacks reporting of statistical significance levels or p-values, missing an opportunity for deeper critical evaluation of the Mozart Effectâs robustness.
The title is presented clearly and the research question is stated prominently, meeting the requirement for a well-structured title page and immediately orienting the reader to the study focus.

The description of neural activation (âmultiple areas get activatedâ) lacks specificity on imaging methods, thresholds, or quantitative activation levels, weakening the scientific rigor of the biological discussion.
The word count is provided and remains under the 4,000-word limit, satisfying the formatting criterion for including an explicit word count on the title page.
Cultural and ethical limitations are acknowledged (e.g., Western bias, stress induction), showing critical engagement with broader implications of cognitive research contexts.
A detailed table of contents with clear section titles and page numbers enhances navigability and demonstrates adherence to conventional extended essay structure.
The discussion of participant selection bias (no musical background) is mentioned but lacks connection to underlying cognitive mechanisms, limiting the critical depth of this limitation.
The writing span test is described clearly as a quantifiable measure of working memory, demonstrating appropriate methodological understanding and precise terminology.
The introduction ends abruptly mid-sentence, disrupting flow and leaving conceptual transitions incomplete. A more polished ending would reinforce coherence and maintain reader engagement.
The definitions of working memory components (phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, episodic buffer) are accurately stated with appropriate citations, demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
The summary of Franklin et al. (2008) is well-integrated, using empirical evidence effectively to support claims about musical training and verbal memory.
The final pageâs long URLs are unformatted and span multiple lines, breaking the visual flow and hindering quick access to online resources; use hyperlink formatting or DOI instead.
The reference to Chabrisâs meta-analysis lacks discussion of effect sizes, heterogeneity, or confidence intervals, missing depth in evaluating contradictory evidence.
Triangulating outcomes with multiple studies (Smith et al., 2010; Habe, 2005) reinforces the argument and demonstrates broad source integration.
The studyâs lack of repeated-measures or long-term follow-up is noted but not justified; a dedicated methodological rationale could strengthen claims about reliability.
Musliu et al.âs multi-condition design is articulated thoroughly, showing the studentâs grasp of how different auditory stimuli engage distinct working memory components.
Highlighting the within-subjects design in Ransdell and Gilroyâs study demonstrates strong critical evaluation of how individual differences are controlled in experimental research.
Although questionnaire limitations are noted, there is no critique of validity or reliability metrics; discussing question design and piloting would deepen the evaluation.
The term âtouching musicâ is informal and undefined; replacing colloquial descriptors with operational definitions would improve precision and academic tone.
The editorial comment contrasting this study with earlier research breaks academic tone; conclusions should be framed analytically rather than personally to maintain objectivity.
The conclusion outlines general future research needs but lacks specificityâsuch as proposed populations, experimental settings, or measurement improvementsâto guide subsequent investigations.
Several URLs in the citation list break across lines and lack consistent formatting, reducing readability and contravening formal reference style conventions.
The reference list includes a broad range of reputable sourcesâfrom primary research articles to authoritative encyclopediasâdemonstrating extensive and relevant literature gathering.
Citation entries show inconsistent style (varying date formats, missing DOIs), indicating a need for uniform application of a single referencing convention.
